---
title: "Chanwe Showcase: ggplot2 + gt"
subtitle: "Tables and plots aligned with chanwe-typst design tokens"
author: "Alejandro Abraham"
date: today
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  chanwe-typst:
    keep-typ: true
    number-sections: true
    section-numbering: "1.1.1"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  dev: ragg_png
  fig-format: png
  fig-dpi: 320
chanwe:
  document: "CHW · DEV"
  edition: "SHOWCASE / 2026"
  volume: "MENDOZA · ARGENTINA"
  chapter: "Design System"
  section: "R Package"
  topic: "chanwer"
  rail-eyebrow: "VISUAL REFERENCE"
  hero-image: "assets/bg_mountains.jpg"
  wordmark: "assets/Logo_Negro.png"
  cover: true
  toc: true
  toc-eyebrow: "Document map"
  toc-title: "Contents"
  toc-lede: "This document showcases all ggplot2 and gt table variants produced by the chanwer R package — 22 figures and 8 tables — styled with the chanwe-typst design tokens: Satoshi body, Archivo Black headings, primary orange #FB3D0E, and the 8-color editorial chart palette."
  abstract-eyebrow: "TLDR;"
  abstract-title: "Visual Reference"
  abstract-takeaway: "chanwer themes are fully aligned with chanwe-typst: same tokens, same fonts, same 8-color editorial palette."
  abstract-text: "This showcase covers every theme variant in the chanwer package — scatter, bar, line, area, distribution, heatmap, faceted, and error-bar figures; gt tables in spacious and compact densities across both background options. All outputs use the typst design tokens directly, ensuring visual consistency between R-generated figures and the surrounding Typst document."
  abstract-status: "Stable · Internal"
  abstract-show:
    - document
    - edition
    - author
    - status
  meta-rows:
    - { label: "Package",  value: "chanwer",          sub: "R design system" }
    - { label: "Section",  value: "Design Tokens",    sub: "chanwe-typst aligned" }
    - { label: "Author",   value: "Chanwe",            sub: "Alejandro Abraham" }
  back-cover: true
  back-cover-tagline-1: "Estrategia Activa,"
  back-cover-tagline-2: "Codo a codo."
  back-cover-cols:
    - { label: "Address",  value: "Via Montenapoleone, 27", sub: "Mendoza · Argentina" }
    - { label: "Web",      value: "chanwe.com.ar",          sub: "contacto@chanwe.com.ar" }
    - { label: "Document", value: "CHW · DEV",              sub: "Showcase / 2026" }
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
devtools::load_all(".")
chanwe_load_fonts()

knitr::opts_chunk$set(dev = "ragg_png", dpi = 320)

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(gt)

# mtcars -----------------------------------------------------------------------
mt <- tibble::as_tibble(mtcars, rownames = "model") |>
    mutate(
        cyl = factor(cyl),
        gear = factor(gear),
        am = factor(am, labels = c("Automatic", "Manual")),
        hp_band = cut(
            hp,
            breaks = c(-Inf, 110, 180, Inf),
            labels = c("Low", "Mid", "High")
        )
    )

summary_tbl <- mt |>
    group_by(cyl, am) |>
    summarise(
        avg_mpg = mean(mpg),
        avg_hp = mean(hp),
        avg_wt = mean(wt),
        n = dplyr::n(),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

cyl_summary <- mt |>
    group_by(cyl) |>
    summarise(
        avg_mpg = mean(mpg),
        sd_mpg = sd(mpg),
        avg_hp = mean(hp),
        avg_wt = mean(wt),
        n = dplyr::n(),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

# economics (ggplot2 built-in) -------------------------------------------------
econ <- economics |>
    filter(date >= as.Date("1990-01-01")) |>
    mutate(unemp_pct = unemploy / pop * 100)

econ_long <- econ |>
    select(date, psavert, unemp_pct) |>
    pivot_longer(-date, names_to = "series", values_to = "value") |>
    mutate(
        series = recode(
            series,
            psavert = "Personal savings rate",
            unemp_pct = "Unemployment rate"
        )
    )

# mpg (ggplot2 built-in) -------------------------------------------------------
mpg_class <- mpg |>
    group_by(class, drv) |>
    summarise(avg_hwy = mean(hwy), n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
    mutate(drv = recode(drv, "4" = "4WD", "f" = "FWD", "r" = "RWD"))

mpg_make <- mpg |>
    group_by(manufacturer) |>
    summarise(
        avg_hwy = mean(hwy),
        avg_cty = mean(cty),
        n = n(),
        .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    slice_max(n, n = 10)

# iris -------------------------------------------------------------------------
iris_tbl <- iris |>
    tibble::as_tibble() |>
    rename(species = Species)

iris_summary <- iris_tbl |>
    group_by(species) |>
    summarise(
        across(everything(), list(mean = mean, sd = sd)),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

# correlation matrix -----------------------------------------------------------
cor_vars <- c("mpg", "cyl", "disp", "hp", "wt", "qsec", "drat")
cor_mat <- cor(mtcars[, cor_vars]) |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("from") |>
    pivot_longer(-from, names_to = "to", values_to = "r") |>
    mutate(
        from = toupper(from),
        to = toupper(to)
    )
```

This document showcases `chanwer` theme functions for `ggplot2` and `gt`,
aligned with chanwe-typst design tokens: Satoshi body, Archivo Black headings,
primary orange `#FB3D0E`, and the 8-color editorial chart palette.

# Brand Color System

## Extended Palette · p15

```{r}
#| label: fig-palette-p15
#| fig-cap: "Extended color ramps — 11 semantic families, 5 shades each (dark → light)."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 7.5
families_df <- tibble::tibble(
    key = c(
        "coral",
        "vermillion",
        "green",
        "magenta",
        "blue",
        "yellow",
        "cyan",
        "mustard",
        "violet",
        "teal",
        "ink"
    ),
    name = c(
        "CORAL",
        "VERMILLION",
        "GREEN",
        "MAGENTA",
        "BLUE",
        "YELLOW",
        "CYAN",
        "MUSTARD",
        "VIOLET",
        "TEAL",
        "INK"
    ),
    sub = c(
        "HOUSE LEAD",
        "ALERT",
        "POSITIVE",
        "CONTRAST",
        "STRUCTURAL",
        "ATTENTION",
        "COOL",
        "EARTHY",
        "EDITORIAL",
        "DEPTH",
        "NEUTRAL ANCHOR"
    )
)

clr <- chanwe_get_colors()

pal_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(nrow(families_df)), function(i) {
    cols <- chanwe_palette(paste0("p15_", families_df$key[i]))
    tibble::tibble(
        family = paste0(
            "**",
            families_df$name[i],
            "**",
            " <span style='font-weight:normal;color:",
            clr[["typst-fg-muted"]],
            ";'>",
            "· ",
            families_df$sub[i],
            "</span>"
        ),
        shade = seq_along(cols),
        hex = unname(cols)
    )
})) |>
    dplyr::mutate(family = factor(family, levels = unique(family)))

ggplot(pal_df, aes(shade, 1, fill = hex)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.6, width = 0.94, height = 1.6) +
    geom_text(
        aes(y = 0.12, label = toupper(hex)),
        size = 1.85,
        family = "JetBrains Mono",
        color = clr[["typst-fg-muted"]],
        vjust = 1
    ) +
    scale_fill_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.05)) +
    facet_wrap(~family, ncol = 3) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.08, 1.85), clip = "off") +
    theme_void(base_family = "Satoshi") +
    theme(
        strip.text = ggtext::element_markdown(
            hjust = 0,
            size = 7.5,
            color = clr[["typst-ink"]],
            margin = margin(b = 5, t = 14)
        ),
        panel.spacing.x = unit(2.0, "lines"),
        panel.spacing.y = unit(2.0, "lines"),
        plot.margin = margin(14, 14, 14, 14),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#FFFFFF", color = NA)
    )
```

## Main Brand · mb

```{r}
#| label: fig-palette-mb
#| fig-cap: "Main brand palette — orange, dark, and beige ramps (100 → 950)."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 3.2
mb_families <- tibble::tibble(
    key = c("orange", "dark", "beige"),
    name = c("ORANGE", "DARK", "BEIGE"),
    sub = c("PRIMARY BRAND", "NEUTRAL ANCHOR", "PAPER / WARM NEUTRAL")
)

clr <- chanwe_get_colors()

mb_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(nrow(mb_families)), function(i) {
    cols <- chanwe_palette(paste0("mb_", mb_families$key[i]))
    tibble::tibble(
        family = paste0(
            "**",
            mb_families$name[i],
            "**",
            " <span style='font-weight:normal;color:",
            clr[["typst-fg-muted"]],
            ";'>",
            "· ",
            mb_families$sub[i],
            "</span>"
        ),
        shade = seq_along(cols),
        hex = unname(cols)
    )
})) |>
    dplyr::mutate(family = factor(family, levels = unique(family)))

ggplot(mb_df, aes(shade, 1, fill = hex)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.6, width = 0.94, height = 1.6) +
    geom_text(
        aes(y = 0.12, label = toupper(hex)),
        size = 1.85,
        family = "JetBrains Mono",
        color = clr[["typst-fg-muted"]],
        vjust = 1
    ) +
    scale_fill_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.02)) +
    facet_wrap(~family, ncol = 1) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.08, 1.85), clip = "off") +
    theme_void(base_family = "Satoshi") +
    theme(
        strip.text = ggtext::element_markdown(
            hjust = 0,
            size = 7.5,
            color = clr[["typst-ink"]],
            margin = margin(b = 5, t = 14)
        ),
        panel.spacing.y = unit(2.0, "lines"),
        plot.margin = margin(14, 14, 14, 14),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#FFFFFF", color = NA)
    )
```

# KPI Summary

:::: {.kpi-grid cols="4"}
::: {.kpi title="VEHICLES" main="32" secondary="mtcars dataset" direction="none"}
:::
::: {.kpi title="AVG MPG" main="20.1" secondary="across all cylinders" direction="none"}
:::
::: {.kpi title="AVG HP" main="146.7" secondary="horsepower" direction="none"}
:::
::: {.kpi title="TRANSMISSION" main="59" unit="%" secondary="automatic" direction="none"}
:::
::::

# Scatter

## Simple scatter

```{r}
#| label: fig-scatter-simple
#| fig-cap: "Fuel economy vs weight, coloured by transmission type."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.2
ggplot(mt, aes(wt, mpg, color = am)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.8, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_color_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5") +
    labs(
        title = "Fuel economy vs weight",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Simple scatter · beige background"),
        x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
        y = "Miles per gallon",
        color = "Transmission",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

## Scatter with regression

```{r}
#| label: fig-scatter-smooth
#| fig-cap: "Scatter with per-group OLS lines. HP band drives colour."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.2
ggplot(mt, aes(wt, mpg, color = hp_band)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.4, alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.65) +
    scale_color_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF") +
    labs(
        title = chanwe_title("Weight vs fuel economy", eyebrow = "REGRESSION"),
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("OLS fit per HP band · white background"),
        x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
        y = "Miles per gallon",
        color = "HP band",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

## Bubble chart

```{r}
#| label: fig-bubble
#| fig-cap: "Bubble chart — engine displacement drives point size."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.4
ggplot(mt, aes(hp, mpg, size = disp, color = cyl)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.75) +
    scale_size_continuous(
        range = c(2, 12),
        guide = guide_legend(
            title = "Displacement",
            override.aes = list(alpha = 0.8)
        )
    ) +
    scale_color_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5") +
    labs(
        title = "Horsepower, efficiency, and displacement",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle(
            "Bubble size = engine displacement (cu.in.)"
        ),
        x = "Horsepower",
        y = "Miles per gallon",
        color = "Cylinders",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

## Continuous colour scale

```{r}
#| label: fig-scatter-continuous
#| fig-cap: "Scatter with continuous colour gradient — quarter-mile time."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.2
ggplot(mt, aes(wt, mpg, color = qsec)) +
    geom_point(size = 3.2, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_color_chanwe_c() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF") +
    labs(
        title = "Fuel economy vs weight",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Continuous colour · quarter-mile time"),
        x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
        y = "Miles per gallon",
        color = "¼-mile (s)",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

# Bar & Column

## Simple bar

```{r}
#| label: fig-bar-simple
#| fig-cap: "Vehicle count by cylinder class."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.2
ggplot(mt, aes(cyl, fill = cyl)) +
    geom_bar(width = 0.65) +
    scale_fill_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF") +
    labs(
        title = "Vehicle count by cylinder",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Simple bar · white background"),
        x = "Cylinders",
        y = "Count",
        fill = "Cylinders",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

## Grouped bar

```{r}
#| label: fig-bar-grouped
#| fig-cap: "Average highway MPG by vehicle class and drive type."
#| fig-width: 7.4
#| fig-height: 4.4
ggplot(mpg_class, aes(class, avg_hwy, fill = drv)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.72) +
    scale_fill_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5") +
    labs(
        title = chanwe_title(
            "Highway efficiency by class",
            eyebrow = "FLEET ANALYSIS"
        ),
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Grouped by drive type · beige background"),
        x = "Vehicle class",
        y = "Avg highway MPG",
        fill = "Drive",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: EPA fuel economy data")
    )
```

## Stacked bar

```{r}
#| label: fig-bar-stacked
#| fig-cap: "Stacked bar — vehicle mix by cylinder and transmission."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.2
mt |>
    count(cyl, am) |>
    ggplot(aes(cyl, n, fill = am)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.65) +
    scale_fill_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF") +
    labs(
        title = "Fleet mix by cylinder and transmission",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Stacked bar · white background"),
        x = "Cylinders",
        y = "Count",
        fill = "Transmission",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

## Horizontal bar

```{r}
#| label: fig-bar-horizontal
#| fig-cap: "Horizontal bar — average highway MPG by manufacturer (top 10)."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.8
ggplot(mpg_make, aes(avg_hwy, reorder(manufacturer, avg_hwy), fill = avg_hwy)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_chanwe_c() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5", legend_position = "none") +
    labs(
        title = "Highway fuel economy by manufacturer",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Top 10 manufacturers by model count"),
        x = "Avg highway MPG",
        y = NULL,
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: EPA fuel economy data")
    )
```

## Lollipop

```{r}
#| label: fig-lollipop
#| fig-cap: "Lollipop — average MPG with ±1 SD error, by cylinder."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.0
ggplot(cyl_summary, aes(x = cyl, y = avg_mpg, color = cyl)) +
    geom_segment(
        aes(xend = cyl, y = avg_mpg - sd_mpg, yend = avg_mpg + sd_mpg),
        linewidth = 0.5,
        alpha = 0.4
    ) +
    geom_point(size = 5) +
    scale_color_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF", legend_position = "none") +
    labs(
        title = chanwe_title("Average MPG ± 1 SD", eyebrow = "DISTRIBUTION"),
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("By cylinder count · white background"),
        x = "Cylinders",
        y = "Miles per gallon",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

# Line & Area

## Single line — time series

```{r}
#| label: fig-line-single
#| fig-cap: "Personal savings rate, US 1990–2015."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.0
ggplot(econ, aes(date, psavert)) +
    geom_line(
        color = chanwe_get_colors()[["chart-coral"]],
        linewidth = 0.75
    ) +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5") +
    labs(
        title = "US personal savings rate",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Single line · 1990–2015"),
        x = NULL,
        y = "Savings rate (%)",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: US Bureau of Economic Analysis")
    )
```

## Multi-line

```{r}
#| label: fig-line-multi
#| fig-cap: "Savings rate and unemployment rate on the same axis."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.2
ggplot(econ_long, aes(date, value, color = series)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_color_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF") +
    labs(
        title = chanwe_title("Savings and unemployment", eyebrow = "MACRO"),
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Two series · white background"),
        x = NULL,
        y = "Rate (%)",
        color = NULL,
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: US BEA & BLS")
    )
```

## Area chart

```{r}
#| label: fig-area
#| fig-cap: "Area chart — unemployment level, US 1990–2015."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.0
ggplot(econ, aes(date, unemploy / 1000)) +
    geom_area(
        fill = chanwe_get_colors()[["chart-blue"]],
        color = chanwe_get_colors()[["chart-blue"]],
        alpha = 0.18,
        linewidth = 0.6
    ) +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5") +
    labs(
        title = "US unemployment level",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Area chart · thousands of persons"),
        x = NULL,
        y = "Unemployed (M)",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: US Bureau of Labor Statistics")
    )
```

## Step line

```{r}
#| label: fig-step
#| fig-cap: "Step chart — median unemployment duration."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.0
ggplot(econ |> filter(date >= as.Date("2000-01-01")), aes(date, uempmed)) +
    geom_step(
        color = chanwe_get_colors()[["chart-green"]],
        linewidth = 0.7
    ) +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF") +
    labs(
        title = "Median weeks unemployed",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Step line · 2000–2015"),
        x = NULL,
        y = "Weeks",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: US Bureau of Labor Statistics")
    )
```

# Distributions

## Histogram

```{r}
#| label: fig-histogram
#| fig-cap: "Histogram of fuel economy (mpg) across all vehicles."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.0
ggplot(mt, aes(mpg, fill = am)) +
    geom_histogram(
        binwidth = 2.5,
        color = "white",
        linewidth = 0.3,
        alpha = 0.85
    ) +
    scale_fill_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5") +
    labs(
        title = "Distribution of fuel economy",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Histogram · bin width 2.5 mpg"),
        x = "Miles per gallon",
        y = "Count",
        fill = "Transmission",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

## Density

```{r}
#| label: fig-density
#| fig-cap: "Kernel density of sepal length by iris species."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.0
ggplot(iris_tbl, aes(Sepal.Length, fill = species, color = species)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.20, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_chanwe_d() +
    scale_color_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF") +
    labs(
        title = chanwe_title("Sepal length distribution", eyebrow = "IRIS"),
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle(
            "Kernel density by species · white background"
        ),
        x = "Sepal length (cm)",
        y = "Density",
        fill = "Species",
        color = "Species",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Fisher, 1936")
    )
```

## Violin + jitter

```{r}
#| label: fig-violin
#| fig-cap: "Violin plot of sepal width by species with raw data overlay."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.2
ggplot(iris_tbl, aes(species, Sepal.Width, fill = species)) +
    geom_violin(alpha = 0.6, trim = TRUE, width = 0.8) +
    geom_jitter(
        width = 0.1,
        size = 1.4,
        alpha = 0.5,
        color = chanwe_get_colors()[["typst-ink"]]
    ) +
    scale_fill_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5", legend_position = "none") +
    labs(
        title = "Sepal width by species",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Violin + jitter · beige background"),
        x = NULL,
        y = "Sepal width (cm)",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Fisher, 1936")
    )
```

## Boxplot inset

::: {.inset-great-figure eyebrow="DISTRIBUTION" title="Fuel efficiency by cylinder and transmission" layout="left" position="right" color="dark" source="Source: Motor Trend, 1974"}

Vehicles with 4 cylinders show the widest spread and highest median efficiency.
8-cylinder vehicles cluster at lower MPG regardless of transmission type.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| dev: ragg_png
#| dev.args: !expr list(background = "transparent")
#| fig-width: 5.5
#| fig-height: 4.2
ggplot(mt, aes(cyl, mpg, fill = am)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.62) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.65, color = "#FFFFFF") +
    scale_fill_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF") +
    theme(
        plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(
            fill = "transparent",
            color = NA
        ),
        panel.background = ggplot2::element_rect(
            fill = "transparent",
            color = NA
        ),
        legend.background = ggplot2::element_rect(
            fill = "transparent",
            color = NA
        ),
        legend.key = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
        text = ggplot2::element_text(color = "#FFFFFF"),
        axis.text = ggplot2::element_text(color = "#FFFFFF"),
        axis.title = ggplot2::element_text(color = "#FFFFFF"),
        strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(color = "#FFFFFF"),
        legend.title = ggplot2::element_text(color = "#FFFFFF"),
        legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(color = "#FFFFFF")
    ) +
    labs(x = "Cylinders", y = "MPG", fill = "Transmission")
```
:::

# Heatmap & Tiles

## Correlation heatmap

```{r}
#| label: fig-heatmap-corr
#| fig-cap: "Pearson correlation matrix for selected mtcars variables."
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 5.2
ggplot(cor_mat, aes(from, to, fill = r)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_text(
        aes(label = round(r, 2)),
        size = 2.8,
        color = ifelse(
            abs(cor_mat$r) > 0.6,
            "white",
            chanwe_get_colors()[["typst-ink"]]
        )
    ) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
        low = chanwe_get_colors()[["chart-blue"]],
        mid = chanwe_get_colors()[["typst-neutral-200"]],
        high = chanwe_get_colors()[["chart-coral"]],
        midpoint = 0,
        limits = c(-1, 1)
    ) +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5", legend_position = "right") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +
    labs(
        title = chanwe_title(
            "Variable correlation matrix",
            eyebrow = "STATISTICS"
        ),
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Pearson r · mtcars variables"),
        x = NULL,
        y = NULL,
        fill = "r",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

## Tile grid — MPG by cylinder and gear

```{r}
#| label: fig-tile-grid
#| fig-cap: "Average MPG tile grid — cylinder × gear combinations."
#| fig-width: 6.0
#| fig-height: 4.2
mt |>
    group_by(cyl, gear) |>
    summarise(avg_mpg = mean(mpg), n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
    ggplot(aes(gear, cyl, fill = avg_mpg)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_text(
        aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", avg_mpg)),
        size = 3.2,
        color = chanwe_get_colors()[["typst-ink"]]
    ) +
    scale_fill_chanwe_c() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF", legend_position = "right") +
    labs(
        title = "Average MPG by cylinder and gear",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Tile grid · white background"),
        x = "Gears",
        y = "Cylinders",
        fill = "Avg MPG",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

# Faceted

## Faceted scatter

```{r}
#| label: fig-facet-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Faceted scatter with regression lines — transmission type panels."
#| fig-width: 7.4
#| fig-height: 4.8
ggplot(mt, aes(wt, mpg, color = hp_band)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.2, alpha = 0.88) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.7) +
    facet_wrap(~am) +
    scale_color_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5") +
    labs(
        title = "Performance profile by transmission",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Faceted by transmission type"),
        x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
        y = "Miles per gallon",
        color = "HP band",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

## Faceted bar

```{r}
#| label: fig-facet-bar
#| fig-cap: "Faceted bar — highway MPG by drive type, faceted by vehicle class."
#| fig-width: 7.4
#| fig-height: 5.0
ggplot(mpg_class, aes(drv, avg_hwy, fill = drv)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.65) +
    facet_wrap(~class, nrow = 2) +
    scale_fill_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF", legend_position = "none") +
    labs(
        title = chanwe_title("Highway MPG by drive type", eyebrow = "BY CLASS"),
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle(
            "Faceted by vehicle class · white background"
        ),
        x = "Drive type",
        y = "Avg highway MPG",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: EPA fuel economy data")
    )
```

# Error Bars & Range

## Mean ± SD by cylinder

```{r}
#| label: fig-errorbar
#| fig-cap: "Mean MPG ± 1 SD with individual observations overlaid."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4.2
ggplot(cyl_summary, aes(cyl, avg_mpg, color = cyl)) +
    geom_errorbar(
        aes(ymin = avg_mpg - sd_mpg, ymax = avg_mpg + sd_mpg),
        width = 0.18,
        linewidth = 0.6
    ) +
    geom_point(size = 4.5) +
    geom_jitter(
        data = mt,
        aes(cyl, mpg, color = cyl),
        width = 0.12,
        size = 1.5,
        alpha = 0.35
    ) +
    scale_color_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#F5F5F5", legend_position = "none") +
    labs(
        title = "Fuel economy mean ± 1 SD",
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle(
            "Error bars with raw data jitter · beige background"
        ),
        x = "Cylinders",
        y = "Miles per gallon",
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Motor Trend, 1974")
    )
```

## Horizontal range — iris petal dimensions

```{r}
#| label: fig-range
#| fig-cap: "Range plot — petal length min/max/mean by species."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.8
iris_range <- iris_tbl |>
    group_by(species) |>
    summarise(
        lo = min(Petal.Length),
        hi = max(Petal.Length),
        mid = mean(Petal.Length),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

ggplot(iris_range, aes(y = species, color = species)) +
    geom_segment(
        aes(x = lo, xend = hi, yend = species),
        linewidth = 1.8,
        alpha = 0.25
    ) +
    geom_point(aes(x = lo), size = 3.5) +
    geom_point(aes(x = hi), size = 3.5) +
    geom_point(aes(x = mid), size = 5, shape = 18) +
    scale_color_chanwe_d() +
    theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF", legend_position = "none") +
    labs(
        title = chanwe_title("Petal length range by species", eyebrow = "IRIS"),
        subtitle = chanwe_subtitle("Min · mean ◆ · max · white background"),
        x = "Petal length (cm)",
        y = NULL,
        caption = chanwe_caption("Source: Fisher, 1936")
    )
```

# GT Tables

## Simple fleet view

```{r}
#| label: tbl-simple
#| tbl-cap: "Fleet overview — top 8 vehicles (white background)."
mt |>
    select(model, mpg, cyl, hp, wt) |>
    slice_head(n = 8) |>
    gt(rowname_col = "model") |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = c(mpg, hp, wt), decimals = 2) |>
    chanwe_gt_header(
        title = "Simple fleet view",
        subtitle = "Top 8 vehicles · mtcars · white background",
        eyebrow = "TABLE · SPACIOUS"
    ) |>
    gt_theme_chanwe(bg_color = "#FFFFFF")
```

## Compact fleet view

```{r}
#| label: tbl-compact
#| tbl-cap: "Fleet overview — compact density (neutral-100 background)."
mt |>
    select(model, mpg, cyl, gear, am) |>
    slice_head(n = 10) |>
    gt(rowname_col = "model") |>
    chanwe_gt_header(
        title = "Compact fleet view",
        subtitle = "Top 10 vehicles · mtcars · neutral-100 background",
        eyebrow = "TABLE · COMPACT"
    ) |>
    gt_theme_chanwe_compact(bg_color = "#F5F5F5")
```

## Complex performance summary

```{r}
#| label: tbl-complex
#| tbl-cap: "Performance summary — grouped by transmission, with spanners."
summary_tbl |>
    gt(groupname_col = "am") |>
    chanwe_gt_header(
        title = "Complex performance summary",
        subtitle = "Averages by cylinder count · grouped by transmission",
        eyebrow = "TABLE · GROUPED",
        caption = "Source · mtcars aggregated in setup chunk"
    ) |>
    gt::tab_spanner(label = "Averages", columns = c(avg_mpg, avg_hp, avg_wt)) |>
    gt::cols_label(
        cyl = "Cyl",
        avg_mpg = "MPG",
        avg_hp = "HP",
        avg_wt = "Weight",
        n = "Rows"
    ) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = c(avg_mpg, avg_hp, avg_wt), decimals = 2) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = n, decimals = 0) |>
    gt_theme_chanwe(variant = "spacious", bg_color = "#FFFFFF")
```

## Spacious + neutral-100

```{r}
#| label: tbl-spacious-beige
#| tbl-cap: "Spacious + neutral-100."
summary_tbl |>
    gt(groupname_col = "am") |>
    chanwe_gt_header(
        title = "Spacious + Neutral-100",
        subtitle = "Grouped by transmission · spacious density",
        eyebrow = "TABLE · SPACIOUS"
    ) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = c(avg_mpg, avg_hp, avg_wt), decimals = 2) |>
    gt_theme_chanwe(variant = "spacious", bg_color = "#F5F5F5")
```

## Spacious + white

```{r}
#| label: tbl-spacious-white
#| tbl-cap: "Spacious + white."
summary_tbl |>
    gt(groupname_col = "am") |>
    chanwe_gt_header(
        title = "Spacious + White",
        subtitle = "Grouped by transmission · spacious density",
        eyebrow = "TABLE · SPACIOUS"
    ) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = c(avg_mpg, avg_hp, avg_wt), decimals = 2) |>
    gt_theme_chanwe(variant = "spacious", bg_color = "#FFFFFF")
```

## Compact + neutral-100

```{r}
#| label: tbl-compact-beige
#| tbl-cap: "Compact + neutral-100."
summary_tbl |>
    gt(groupname_col = "am") |>
    chanwe_gt_header(
        title = "Compact + Neutral-100",
        subtitle = "Grouped by transmission · compact density",
        eyebrow = "TABLE · COMPACT"
    ) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = c(avg_mpg, avg_hp, avg_wt), decimals = 2) |>
    gt_theme_chanwe(variant = "compact", bg_color = "#F5F5F5")
```

## Compact + white

```{r}
#| label: tbl-compact-white
#| tbl-cap: "Compact + white."
summary_tbl |>
    gt(groupname_col = "am") |>
    chanwe_gt_header(
        title = "Compact + White",
        subtitle = "Grouped by transmission · compact density",
        eyebrow = "TABLE · COMPACT"
    ) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = c(avg_mpg, avg_hp, avg_wt), decimals = 2) |>
    gt_theme_chanwe(variant = "compact", bg_color = "#FFFFFF")
```

## Iris species comparison

```{r}
#| label: tbl-iris
#| tbl-cap: "Iris species — mean dimensions with footnote annotations."
iris_tbl |>
    group_by(species) |>
    summarise(
        sl_mean = mean(Sepal.Length),
        sl_sd = sd(Sepal.Length),
        sw_mean = mean(Sepal.Width),
        sw_sd = sd(Sepal.Width),
        pl_mean = mean(Petal.Length),
        pl_sd = sd(Petal.Length),
        pw_mean = mean(Petal.Width),
        pw_sd = sd(Petal.Width),
        n = n(),
        .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    gt() |>
    chanwe_gt_header(
        title = "Iris species morphology",
        subtitle = "Mean ± SD across four measurements · n = 50 per species",
        eyebrow = "TABLE · SPECIES COMPARISON",
        caption = "Source · Fisher, 1936 — Anderson iris data"
    ) |>
    gt::tab_spanner(label = "Sepal length (cm)", columns = c(sl_mean, sl_sd)) |>
    gt::tab_spanner(label = "Sepal width (cm)", columns = c(sw_mean, sw_sd)) |>
    gt::tab_spanner(label = "Petal length (cm)", columns = c(pl_mean, pl_sd)) |>
    gt::tab_spanner(label = "Petal width (cm)", columns = c(pw_mean, pw_sd)) |>
    gt::cols_label(
        species = "Species",
        sl_mean = "Mean",
        sl_sd = "SD",
        sw_mean = "Mean",
        sw_sd = "SD",
        pl_mean = "Mean",
        pl_sd = "SD",
        pw_mean = "Mean",
        pw_sd = "SD",
        n = "n"
    ) |>
    gt::fmt_number(columns = -c(species, n), decimals = 2) |>
    gt::tab_footnote(
        footnote = "Virginica shows the largest petal dimensions.",
        locations = gt::cells_body(
            columns = pl_mean,
            rows = species == "virginica"
        )
    ) |>
    gt_theme_chanwe(variant = "spacious", bg_color = "#F5F5F5")
```
